name: Brunei

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Brunei (brun1242).

notes: Skeleton. Brunei Malay is a Malayic (Malayo-Polynesian) Austronesian language written with a Latin alphabet on the standard Malay/Rumi convention. The grapheme map follows Malay orthography: the digraphs ng, ny, sy, kh, gh; c=voiceless and j=voiced palatal affricates; and the vowels, with e mapped to schwa (the letter e also spells /e/ in some words, a distinction the orthography does not disambiguate). Brunei Malay characteristically reduces the vowel system toward /a i u/, so mid vowels o and e overlap with high vowels in native words; this conditioned variation is not encoded. Final /k/ and orthographic q realized as glottal stop, and any phonemic glottal stop, are left unencoded, as are stress and length. Loan letters f, v, z, q are mapped to their nearest Malay values.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Brunei (brun1242)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
